symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe epigastric pain boring through to the back
symptoms.character: severe, constant, boring through to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Epigastric pain radiating to the back is a cardinal feature of acute pancreatitis.
Sudden severe epigastric pain is also the hallmark of perforation — cannot be distinguished on history alone.
Epigastric pain can be an atypical cardiac presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain relief on sitting up and leaning forward

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional relief leaning forward is a classic feature of retroperitoneal pancreatic inflammation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profuse vomiting with inability to tolerate oral fluids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Profuse vomiting and early ileus accompany acute pancreatitis; also drives dehydration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known gallstones (cholelithiasis) with minimal alcohol intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gallstones are the commonest cause of acute pancreatitis in Australia; low alcohol makes alcoholic pancreatitis less likely, pointing to a biliary cause.
Gallstones are also the cause of cholecystitis — a competing biliary diagnosis.
Non-specific systemic upset; supports a serious cause without discriminating between them.
Diaphoresis with epigastric pain keeps atypical ACS on the differential.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked epigastric tenderness with voluntary guarding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal epigastric tenderness supports pancreatitis; a sealed bedside finding the AI cannot obtain.
Absence of rigid board-like abdomen / rebound argued against perforation in-person — the very examination telehealth cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal distension with diminished bowel sounds (early ileus)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Early ileus accompanies pancreatitis; requires auscultation/palpation unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative Murphy's, Cullen's and Grey Turner's signs; no scleral icterus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative Murphy's sign argues against cholecystitis — a bedside test the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (heart rate ~110) with borderline low-grade temperature

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia reflects pain, systemic inflammation and hypovolaemia — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers systemic upset from reported sweating/shakiness, not a measured pulse.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum lipase greater than three times the upper limit of normal (anticipated)

investigation_details.expected_result: elevated (>3x upper limit of normal expected in acute pancreatitis)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis of pancreatitis cannot be biochemically confirmed; severity and progression cannot be tracked.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lipase >3x ULN with typical pain confirms acute pancreatitis — a hospital blood test, never available in telehealth in real time.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden severe epigastric-to-back pain with positional relief, intractable vomiting and gallstones is a coherent biliary-pancreatitis picture — but still needs lipase and imaging to confirm and needs exam to exclude perforation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms pancreatitis when >3x ULN with typical pain; a hospital/pathology test the AI arranges via ED referral, not one it can perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bedside abdominal examination by a clinician
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess for peritonism / rigid board-like abdomen and exclude a perforated viscus — the core examination telehealth cannot perform.
To exclude an atypical inferior/epigastric myocardial infarction in a patient with epigastric pain and diaphoresis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: FBC, CRP, LFTs, electrolytes, urea/creatinine, lipase, lactate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Severity stratification, biliary cause (LFTs) and resuscitation guidance — all hospital bloods.
Confirm gallstones/biliary obstruction and assess pancreatic complications; imaging the AI cannot obtain remotely.
Prophylactic antibiotics are not indicated in uncomplicated acute pancreatitis; the AI should not recommend them.
